title: Disabling ASP.NET Request Validation feature is security-sensitive
description: Disabling ASP.NET "Request Validation" feature is security-sensitiveAnalyze your codeintentionality-completesecuritySecurity HotspotcweASP.NET 1.1+ comes with a feature calledRequest Validation, preventing the server to accept content containing un-encoded HTML. This feature comes as a first protection layer against Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) attacks and act as a simple Web Application Firewall (WAF) rejecting requests potentially containing malicious content.While this feature is not a silver bullet to prevent all XSS attacks, it helps to catch basic ones. It will for example prevent<script type="text/javascript" src="https://malicious.domain/payload.js">to reach your Controller.Note:Request Validationfeature being only available for ASP.NET, no Security Hotspot is raised on ASP.NET Core
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant:
<%@ Page ValidateRequest="false" %>
<script runat="server">
    protected void Page_Load(object sender, EventArgs e)
    {
        string userInput = Request.QueryString["data"];
        Response.Write(userInput); // Noncompliant: No validation
    }
</script>

logic.reasoning: DisablingASP.NET request validation removes protection against XSS attacks. The [ValidateInput(false)] attribute explicitly disables this security feature.
